task_completion.description: Create a new role named 'compliance_auditor' that inherits from the 'user' role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Splunk Settings > Roles page or role creation screen, the role name 'compliance_auditor' is visible, and the 'user' role is selected under the inheritance settings.

task_completion.description: Restrict the 'compliance_auditor' role's access exclusively to the 'security_logs' index for both allowed and default indexes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the role configuration screen (Indexes tab), 'security_logs' is selected as both an Included (allowed) and Default index, and no other indexes are selected.

task_completion.description: Apply a search filter to the 'compliance_auditor' role that limits results to events containing the keyword 'Accepted'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the role configuration screen (Restrictions or Search filter section), the keyword 'Accepted' is entered into the search filter field.
In the Splunk Settings > Users page or user creation screen, the username 'auditor_joe' is visible.

task_completion.description: Assign the user 'auditor_joe' exclusively to the 'compliance_auditor' role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the user configuration screen or the Users list, 'compliance_auditor' is shown as the ONLY role assigned to 'auditor_joe' (the default 'user' role should not be directly assigned if 'compliance_auditor' is the only one requested).

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web GUI to configure roles and users rather than executing terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation through the Splunk Web interface (Settings > Roles, Settings > Users) rather than a terminal window running curl or REST API commands.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Splunk interface elements (black navigation bar, Splunk logo, settings menus) are clearly visible.

integrity.description: Agent entered the required values into the correct configuration fields rather than just typing them into a search bar or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Values like 'compliance_auditor', 'security_logs', and 'Accepted' are typed into their respective dedicated form fields in the Splunk settings menus.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The role must be named exactly 'compliance_auditor'. The user must be named exactly 'auditor_joe'. The allowed and default index must be restricted to 'security_logs'. The search filter applied to the role must contain the keyword 'Accepted'.
